title: National Airline Bankruptcy
description: The country’s largest airline faces bankruptcy, threatening thousands of jobs.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: A bailout protects jobs and prevents economic shock. Past crises showed how vital airlines are to stability.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: Bailouts reward mismanagement. Bankruptcy allows natural correction.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Bankruptcy risks instability. Air travel is critical infrastructure.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Nationalization is too extreme — it scares investors.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: And bailouts scare taxpayers. Freedom means letting markets correct.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Freedom doesn’t matter if the aviation system collapses.
